skills.name: readme
skills.description: Reference and guides for extending Fliplet apps with the JS API, REST API, and custom components, themes, and menus.

skills.name: aab-create-p12-certificate
skills.description: Generate a P12 signing key from an Apple Enterprise account to supply to Fliplet's Automated App Build system.

skills.name: ai-powered-development-with-cursor
skills.description: How to install and use the Fliplet VS Code extension inside Cursor to generate, refactor, and debug Fliplet app code with AI assistance.

skills.name: ajax-cross-domain
skills.description: How Fliplet apps handle AJAX cross-domain requests, common CORS errors, and the studio and app domains allowed by default.

skills.name: analytics-documentation
skills.description: Reference list of analytics event types Fliplet emits for page views, navigation, app management, sharing, and component interactions.

skills.name: api-documentation
skills.description: Javascript-based SDK to interact with core functions and components in your Fliplet Apps.

skills.name: api-components-accordions
skills.description: The Accordion component supports query parameters to open specific accordions when linking to a screen.

skills.name: api-components-charts
skills.description: Use the Charts JS API to retrieve chart instances on a screen and update their data, labels, and series at runtime.

skills.name: api-components-chat
skills.description: These public JS APIs will be automatically available in your screens once a **Chat layout** component is used. You can also include these by manually adding the `fliplet-chat` dependency to your app.

skills.name: api-components-data-directory
skills.description: These public JS APIs will be automatically available in your screens once a **Data Directory** component is dropped into such screens.

skills.name: api-components-dynamic-container
skills.description: The dynamic container allows you to dynamically load data from a data source in a screen. It is useful when you want to display a list of records in a screen using the [list repeater](/API/components…

skills.name: api-components-email-verification
skills.description: Use the Email Verification JS API to retrieve the component instance on a screen and trigger verification flows for a given email address.

skills.name: api-components-form-builder
skills.description: The following JS APIs are available in a screen once a **Form** component is dropped into the screen.

skills.name: api-components-interactive-graphics
skills.description: These public JS APIs are available in screens where an **Interactive Graphics** component is present.

skills.name: api-components-list-from-data-source
skills.description: Configure templates, hooks, and query parameters for the List (from Data Source) component, which renders summary and detail views backed by a data source.

skills.name: api-components-list-repeater
skills.description: The list repeater component is a container for a list of records. It is used to display a list of records from a data source.

skills.name: api-components-login
skills.description: The login component fires hooks at key points in the authentication lifecycle. These allow you to run custom code after a user successfully logs in or when a returning user's session is re-validated.

skills.name: api-components-menu-bottom-icon-bar
skills.description: Use a query parameter to highlight the active item when linking into a screen that uses the Bottom Icon Bar menu component.

skills.name: api-components-push-notifications
skills.description: These public JS APIs will be automatically available in your screens once **Push Notifications** have been enabled for your app.

skills.name: api-components-record-container
skills.description: The record container allows you to display one record from a data source in a screen. It is useful when you want to display a single record in a screen, for example a contact card.

skills.name: api-core-ai
skills.description: Build AI features in Fliplet apps with chat, completions, streaming, and task helpers — backed by OpenAI (GPT, o-series) or Google Gemini models via direct proxy.

skills.name: api-core-analytics
skills.description: Enable, disable, and check analytics tracking, and record custom app events and page views from JavaScript.

skills.name: api-core-api
skills.description: Make authenticated HTTP requests to Fliplet APIs with automatic URL construction, caching, offline queueing, and error handling.

skills.name: api-core-app-actions-v2
skills.description: Deprecated App Actions V2 — configure a pipeline of functions that runs on-device or in the cloud, ad hoc or on a schedule. Migrate to V3.

skills.name: api-core-app-actions-v3
skills.description: Write and run JavaScript code directly on the server or client to perform automations, scheduled tasks and on-demand operations.

skills.name: api-core-app-actions
skills.description: Deprecated App Actions V1 — run app screens automatically on a schedule or on demand in the cloud. Migrate to V3.

skills.name: api-core-app
skills.description: Retrieve the current app's public slug, build shareable screen URLs, and access app-level settings from JavaScript.

skills.name: api-core-apps
skills.description: List the Fliplet apps the current user can access, and filter between legacy V1 and modern V2 apps.

skills.name: api-core-biometrics
skills.description: Check biometric availability and verify users with Face ID, Touch ID, or fingerprint inside native Fliplet apps.

skills.name: api-core-cache
skills.description: Run async operations once and memoize their results, with optional expiry and background refresh.

skills.name: api-core-encode
skills.description: Encode strings as base64 and double-encode URL query parameters safely for transport.

skills.name: api-core-environment
skills.description: Read environment variables such as `appId`, `appName`, `mode`, and `apiUrl` from the current runtime.

skills.name: api-core-error
skills.description: Turn any error response or object into a human-readable message by scanning common error properties.

skills.name: api-core-hooks
skills.description: Register callbacks that run before or after key app events (e.g. form submit), with sync or async Promise handlers.

skills.name: api-core-localization
skills.description: Customize your component to localize to different languages and regions.

skills.name: api-core-misc
skills.description: Utility helpers on the global `Fliplet` object: `Fliplet.compile()` for templating and `Fliplet.guid()` for unique IDs.

skills.name: api-core-modal
skills.description: Show confirmation, alert, and prompt dialogs from widget interfaces in Fliplet Studio, powered by [Bootbox](http://bootboxjs.com/) — see its documentation for the full set of options you can pass.

skills.name: api-core-navigate
skills.description: Navigate the app to a new page, screen, document or URL

skills.name: api-core-navigator
skills.description: Detect online/offline state, listen for connectivity changes, and wait for the device to be ready.

skills.name: api-core-notifications
skills.description: Check notification support, request permission, and send local device notifications from JavaScript.

skills.name: api-core-organizations
skills.description: List the current user's organizations and fetch audit logs with filters for type, date range, app, and session.

skills.name: api-core-overview
skills.description: Fliplet Core is the JS library bundled into every app screen — storage, navigation, user, API, and dozens of device helpers.

skills.name: api-core-profile
skills.description: Read and write namespaced user profile attributes (email, name, company, phone) and fetch the device UUID.

skills.name: api-core-registry
skills.description: Store and retrieve runtime values and functions by key so components can share state and helpers.

skills.name: api-core-screens
skills.description: List app screens, get the current screen's public URL, and build shareable URLs for any screen by ID.

skills.name: api-core-storage
skills.description: Persist JSON-serializable values to device or browser storage, scoped globally or to the current app.

skills.name: api-core-studio
skills.description: Emit events to Fliplet Studio and listen for events from it when building widget interfaces.

skills.name: api-core-user
skills.description: Get and set the current user's auth token, profile details, and preferences, and sign the user out.

skills.name: api-core-widget
skills.description: Access widget instance IDs, settings, and data, and coordinate save and ready events between widget and interface.

skills.name: api-datasources-joins
skills.description: Fetch related rows from multiple data sources in a single query using named joins, like SQL joins.

skills.name: api-datasources-query-operators
skills.description: Reference for MongoDB/Sift-style operators (`$eq`, `$gt`, `$in`, `$regex`, …) usable in `connection.find()` `where` clauses.

skills.name: api-datasources-views
skills.description: Define named, session-aware filters on a data source so each user or group sees only the rows that apply to them.

skills.name: api-fliplet-audio-player
skills.description: Embed an audio player UI in a screen by tagging HTML elements with audio URLs; the framework auto-initializes players on screen load.

skills.name: api-fliplet-audio
skills.description: Play, pause, stop, and seek audio files on device or from a URL in Fliplet apps via the Audio namespace.

skills.name: api-fliplet-communicate
skills.description: Send email, SMS, push notifications, and share URLs from a Fliplet app using a single Communicate namespace.

skills.name: api-fliplet-content
skills.description: (Returns **`Promise`**)

skills.name: api-fliplet-database
skills.description: Low-level helper for reading and querying JSON data from a local file as a database; used internally by Fliplet.DataSources.

skills.name: api-fliplet-datasources
skills.description: Connect to, query, insert, update, and delete records in Fliplet Data Sources from inside an app. All methods are promise-based.

skills.name: api-fliplet-encryption
skills.description: Automatically encrypt and decrypt selected Data Source columns on-device by registering a private key and column list.

skills.name: api-fliplet-gamify
skills.description: Configure gamification logic for your apps.

skills.name: api-fliplet-media
skills.description: Browse folders, upload and manage files, and download media to devices via the Fliplet Media namespace.

skills.name: api-fliplet-notifications
skills.description: Read, send, and schedule in-app and push notifications in Fliplet apps, with support for scopes, read receipts, and badge counts.

skills.name: api-fliplet-oauth2
skills.description: The `fliplet-oauth2` package contains helpers for standardizing requests to OAuth2 web services with a client-side integration.

skills.name: api-fliplet-page
skills.description: Utilities for interacting with the current Fliplet screen — including smooth-scrolling to an element with configurable duration and offsets.

skills.name: api-fliplet-payments
skills.description: Adds payments functionality in your Fliplet apps using Stripe and our easy-to-use JS API.

skills.name: api-fliplet-profile-content
skills.description: (Returns **`Promise`**)

skills.name: api-fliplet-session
skills.description: Read, write, and clear the current user session — including cached offline sessions — for authentication state in Fliplet apps.

skills.name: api-fliplet-table
skills.description: Fliplet.UI.Table is a powerful and flexible table component that provides features like sorting, searching, pagination, row selection, expandable rows, and custom rendering capabilities.

skills.name: api-fliplet-themes
skills.description: Read the list of available app themes and access the current theme's settings and widget-instance values at runtime.

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-actions
skills.description: (Returns **`Promise`**)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-address
skills.description: The Address field is a customizable component that integrates with Google Maps to enable address lookup. It allows users to search and select addresses using Google's autocomplete and retrieve locati…

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-datepicker
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-daterange
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-number
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-panzoom
skills.description: These public JS APIs will be automatically available in your screen once an Interactive graphics component is dropped into a screen. You can also add the dependency `fliplet-ui-panzoom` in Developer…

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-rangeslider
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-timepicker
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-timerange
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-toast-error
skills.description: (Returns **`undefined`**)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-toast
skills.description: (Returns **`Promise`**)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui-typeahead
skills.description: (Returns `Object`)

skills.name: api-fliplet-ui
skills.description: The `Fliplet.UI` namespace contains several classes for creating UI managed by Fliplet. Where appropriate, the UI would come with the following benefits:

skills.name: api-helpers-components
skills.description: Package a Fliplet Helper as a reusable widget-framework component so it can be installed and dropped into apps like any first-party component.

skills.name: api-helpers-dynamic-components
skills.description: Helpers can be dropped into what we refer as "dynamic components". These include the **Dynamic container** and **List Repeater** components. Such components supports reactive data binding between the…

skills.name: api-helpers-example-decision-tree
skills.description: Build a decision tree using the Fliplet Helper library.

skills.name: api-helpers-example-question
skills.description: A worked example showing how to build a Helper that presents a multiple-choice question and reveals whether the selected answer is correct.

skills.name: api-helpers-fields
skills.description: Pass attributes to a Helper via the HTML `field` element and read them inside the Helper via `this.fields.<name>`.

skills.name: api-helpers-hooks
skills.description: Run code at key points in a Helper's lifecycle using the `beforeReady` and `ready` render hooks.

skills.name: api-helpers-interface-fields
skills.description: Define the fields shown in a Helper's Fliplet Studio configuration interface, including their type, label, validation, and default value.

skills.name: api-helpers-interface-hooks
skills.description: Run code when a Helper's configuration interface initializes, becomes ready, or is about to save.

skills.name: api-helpers-interface-libraries
skills.description: Include [Fliplet-approved](/Fliplet-approved-libraries.html) or third-party JavaScript and CSS libraries in a Helper's configuration interface via the `dependencies` array.

skills.name: api-helpers-interface-methods
skills.description: Use `find`, `findOne`, and `children` inside a Helper's configuration interface to access nested child Helper instances.

skills.name: api-helpers-interface
skills.description: Define a configuration UI for your Helper so users can set field values for each instance from within Fliplet Studio.

skills.name: api-helpers-libraries
skills.description: Declare a Helper's runtime dependencies by listing [Fliplet-approved](/Fliplet-approved-libraries.html) or third-party JS/CSS libraries in its `render.dependencies` array.

skills.name: api-helpers-methods
skills.description: Define instance methods on a Helper and call Helper class methods such as `find` and `findOne` anywhere in the app lifecycle.

skills.name: api-helpers-overview
skills.description: Helpers are a UI framework for building custom components in Fliplet apps using JavaScript, with a configuration interface in Fliplet Studio.

skills.name: api-helpers-quickstart
skills.description: Create your first Fliplet Helper by defining its name, template, and configuration object in screen or global JavaScript.

skills.name: api-helpers-references-constructor
skills.description: Reference for `Fliplet.Helper()`: the constructor used to define a new Helper in a screen or globally across an app.

skills.name: api-helpers-references-fields
skills.description: Reference for `Fliplet.Helper.field()`: call this from a Helper's configuration interface to read or write the value of another field.

skills.name: api-helpers-references-query
skills.description: Reference for `Fliplet.Helper` query methods used to find Helper instances on the current screen from app code or from a configuration interface.

skills.name: api-helpers-style
skills.description: Style a Helper using standard `class` and `style` HTML attributes — they are passed through to the rendered element rather than treated as Helper fields.

skills.name: api-helpers-templates
skills.description: Every Helper defines an HTML template that renders it on screen, with support for variable binding, conditional blocks, and loops.

skills.name: api-helpers-views
skills.description: Define named rich-content views inside a Helper so app builders can drop approved child Helpers into specific regions of its layout.

skills.name: api-integrations-sso-saml2
skills.description: Add enterprise SSO to a Fliplet app with the SAML2 component, using an identity provider metadata XML exchange.

skills.name: api-libraries-handlebars
skills.description: All screens of your Fliplet app already include [Handlebars `2.15.2`](https://handlebarsjs.com/) hence you can start using it straight away. Here's a quick example following the official Handlebars d…

skills.name: api-like-buttons
skills.description: Embed a one-tap like button on any screen element, backed by a Data Source that records likes per content ID.

skills.name: api-providers-data-source
skills.description: The Data Source provider lets users pick or create a data source for a component, including default columns, entries, and access rules.

skills.name: api-providers-email
skills.description: The Email provider lets users compose and configure an email template (subject, body, recipients, headers) that can then be sent via `Fliplet.Communicate.sendEmail()`.

skills.name: api-providers-file-picker
skills.description: The File Picker provider lets users select one or more files from Fliplet's File Manager, optionally scoped by file type or restricted to single-select.

skills.name: api-providers-link-action
skills.description: The Link Action provider offers a reusable UI for configuring an action (e.g. navigate to screen, open URL, send email) that another feature will trigger on a condition it defines.

skills.name: app-security
skills.description: Fliplet apps can have each of their screens and data sources secured so that they can only be accessed when certain conditions are met. Take the following example:

skills.name: approved-libraries
skills.description: The JavaScript libraries Fliplet pre-approves for app development, why you should prefer them over custom ones, and when to reach for each.

skills.name: async-await
skills.description: As you might know most of Fliplet JS APIs use **Promises** when the result of such operation cannot be read synchronously. For example when reading data from the server or the device storage.

skills.name: best-practises
skills.description: Got stuck building components or found some nasty bugs? Here's a few tips that might help you dealing with basic issues during the development of components on our platform.

skills.name: building-components
skills.description: How to scaffold, run, and develop a Fliplet component locally using the CLI, including the generated file layout and the local dev server.

skills.name: building-functions
skills.description: How to scaffold and develop an app action function with the CLI, declare its dependencies, and configure it via widget.json.

skills.name: building-menus
skills.description: How to scaffold a Fliplet menu widget, configure its position and settings via menu.json, and develop its template and assets.

skills.name: building-themes
skills.description: How to scaffold a Fliplet theme, configure its customizable settings from Studio, and preview sample HTML during local development.

skills.name: changelog
skills.description: Changelog notes giving a summary of recent significant changes to the documentation.

skills.name: cloning-widgets
skills.description: How to use the fliplet clone and fliplet list CLI commands to download the source of a published component, menu, or theme to your machine.

skills.name: code-api-patterns
skills.description: Overview of the main Fliplet JS API categories (Data Sources, Users, Media, Navigation) and when to pick each one.

skills.name: coding-standards
skills.description: Coding and documentation standards used across Fliplet APIs, components, and examples, so both humans and AI tools produce consistent Fliplet code.

skills.name: component-events
skills.description: When components are moved around the page in *Edit* mode, Fliplet provides a set of events to manage the user interaction.

skills.name: components-build-output
skills.description: Components generally output HTML code when dropped into the page. Their output is compiled with [Handlebars](http://handlebarsjs.com/) using the `build.html` file from your component.

skills.name: components-definition
skills.description: The `widget.json` file defines your component as well as the **dependencies** and **assets** it needs in order to run.

skills.name: components-interface
skills.description: Components usually define interfaces to let their instances **save settings**.

skills.name: components-using-providers
skills.description: Components can display providers to get specific data from the system or need a particular piece of functionality to be added.

skills.name: context-targeting
skills.description: Fliplet uses [Modernizr](https://modernizr.com/) (`v3.5.0`) to help developers target users with specific device capabilities and contexts. This lets you target users via selectors and flags provided…

skills.name: custom-headers
skills.description: Fliplet allows you to set custom headers for your apps. This feature is useful for enhancing security, controlling resource loading, and adding custom HTTP headers to your applications.

skills.name: custom-template-for-components
skills.description: How to override a component's default template with custom HTML and Handlebars in the new container-based drag-and-drop system.

skills.name: data-flow
skills.description: Architecture diagram showing how data flows between Fliplet Studio, Fliplet servers, app clients, and connected data sources.

skills.name: data-integration-service
skills.description: Fliplet Agent (Data integration service) is a command line utility to synchronize data to and from Fliplet Servers.

skills.name: data-source-hooks
skills.description: Data Source hooks allow your app's backend to perform certain operations such as **sending emails** or **push notifications** when certain conditions occur, e.g. inserting or updating a Data Source E…

skills.name: data-source-security
skills.description: Secure your Data Sources with access rules, data requirements, and custom JavaScript security rules.

skills.name: dependencies-and-assets
skills.description: Both **components** and **themes** can specify a list of dependencies and local assets which should be used when rendering it.

skills.name: event-emitter
skills.description: Components interfaces can send events to Fliplet Studio using a event emitter bus provided with the `fliplet-core` dependency.

skills.name: execution-flow
skills.description: The rendering and hook lifecycle Fliplet apps go through before a screen is shown to the user, and where to safely run custom code within it.

skills.name: file-security
skills.description: Secure files and folders in your Fliplet apps with access rules and custom JavaScript security rules.

skills.name: fliplet-approved-libraries
skills.description: Reference list of every JavaScript library Fliplet pre-approves in apps, including versions and optional add-on libraries.

skills.name: fliplet-sdk
skills.description: The Fliplet SDK enables you to use all the JS APIs outside of our environment. This means you can use them on any client-side code which is not sitting on Fliplet's domains.

skills.name: integrate-with-external-api
skills.description: Call third-party REST APIs from a Fliplet app using jQuery AJAX helpers, including CORS considerations.

skills.name: introduction
skills.description: Overview of the Fliplet developer stack (JavaScript, SASS, Handlebars) and the kinds of apps, components, themes, and menus you can build on it.

skills.name: javascript-coding-standards
skills.description: Recommended ES6+ patterns for new Fliplet code and ES5 patterns for legacy apps, covering promises, async/await, and API integration.

skills.name: js-apis
skills.description: How the Fliplet JS APIs (SDK, not a framework) let you interact with data, screens, users, and components from Studio custom code, themes, and widgets.

skills.name: native-framework-changelog
skills.description: Release notes for the Fliplet iOS and Android native frameworks — rebuild your app against a newer version to unlock new features.

skills.name: open-source
skills.description: Where to find Fliplet's open-source code, pre-built app solutions, screen templates, community code examples, and developer documentation.

skills.name: organization-audit-log-types
skills.description: Reference table of every audit log type emitted for Fliplet organizations, queryable via the JS or REST API.

skills.name: platform-android
skills.description: How to detect Android at runtime and enable the hardware back button inside a Fliplet app.

skills.name: platform-ios
skills.description: How to detect iOS at runtime and supply a manual P12 certificate when using Fliplet's Automated App Build with an Apple Enterprise account.

skills.name: publishing
skills.description: How to publish Fliplet components, themes, and menus to the Fliplet platform using the CLI's publish command.

skills.name: quickstart
skills.description: Install Node.js and the Fliplet CLI so you can develop and test components, themes, and menus on your machine.

skills.name: rate-limiting-for-api
skills.description: If you’re integrating your app or service with Fliplet APIs, you should be mindful in relation to how frequent your requests are hitting our APIs. Our infrastructure is set up to automatically thrott…

skills.name: reduce-app-bundle-size
skills.description: Blacklist assets from your Fliplet app bundle to shrink it for App Store and Google Play submission.

skills.name: rest-api-documentation
skills.description: Index of Fliplet REST endpoints for Data Sources, Media, Notifications, Apps, and more — intended for third-party integrations.

skills.name: rest-api-authenticate
skills.description: All Fliplet REST API requests are made over HTTPS to a regional endpoint (`api.fliplet.com`, `us.api.fliplet.com`, or `ca.api.fliplet.com`) and must include an auth token.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-app-analytics
skills.description: The App Analytics REST API lets you read your app's analytics data.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-app-subscriptions
skills.description: The App Subscriptions REST API lets you read and manage your app's users subscribed via push notifications.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-apps
skills.description: The Apps REST API lets external integrations list, read, create, update, and delete Fliplet apps that the auth token has access to.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-communicate
skills.description: The Communicate REST API lets external integrations send emails and SMS from a Fliplet app, subject to per-account rate limits.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-datasources
skills.description: The Data Sources REST API lets external integrations read and modify the data sources belonging to a Fliplet app or organization.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-media
skills.description: The Media REST API lets external integrations upload, list, and manage media files and folders scoped to a Fliplet app or organization.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-notifications
skills.description: The Notifications REST API lets external integrations create, schedule, and publish in-app and push notifications to Fliplet app users.

skills.name: rest-api-fliplet-organizations
skills.description: The Organizations REST API lets external integrations read audit logs and manage resources belonging to a Fliplet organization.

skills.name: testing-components
skills.description: Run and write tests for a Fliplet component using the CLI's test command, backed by Mocha, Chai, and Puppeteer.

skills.name: theme-settings-in-css
skills.description: Our theme is built with reusability in mind, therefore we used SCSS variables for all the settings so that you can use them in your custom CSS.

skills.name: theming-appearance
skills.description: Expose configurable colors, fonts, and other CSS properties in Fliplet Studio's theme settings UI via your component's widget.json.

skills.name: ui-guidelines-build
skills.description: Recommended Bootstrap-based styles for buttons, forms, and typography in a Fliplet component's user-facing output.

skills.name: ui-guidelines-interface
skills.description: Recommended Bootstrap-based styles for buttons, forms, and typography in a Fliplet component's Studio settings interface.

skills.name: upcoming
skills.description: Tracker of Fliplet features recently shipped or in beta, linking to their developer documentation.

skills.name: urls-and-ip-addresses
skills.description: Domains and ports that Fliplet Studio, web apps, native apps, and the Data Integration Service need reachable through a corporate firewall.

skills.name: vs-code-extension-setup-usage
skills.description: Install, authenticate, and use the Fliplet VS Code extension to develop Fliplet apps directly from your editor.
